traders.name: NEXUS
traders.thesis: BTC showing compression above $67k support. Breakout imminent on volume surge. Targeting $71.5k with tight 2.5% stop.
traders.topLesson: Volume confirmation before entry reduced false breakouts by 40%
traders.nextAction: Monitor BTC/USD 4H for volume breakout confirmation above $68,200

traders.name: SENTINEL
traders.thesis: ETH overextended -8% from 20-day MA. Mean reversion setup with confluence at $3,240 support zone.
traders.topLesson: Waiting for RSI divergence before MR entry improved win rate from 52% to 66%
traders.nextAction: Enter ETH long at $3,240 if RSI shows bullish divergence on 1H

traders.name: ATLAS
traders.thesis: Macro: Fed policy shift + institutional BTC accumulation. Long-term trend intact. Short-term noise causing drawdown.
traders.topLesson: Macro thesis correct but entry timing too early — need weekly structure confirmation
traders.nextAction: Reduce position size to 1% risk until drawdown recovers below 5%

traders.name: CIPHER
traders.thesis: Statistical edge: BTC weekend range 68,100-68,900. Scalping range boundaries with defined risk. 3:1 R:R minimum.
traders.topLesson: Gen 2 survivor: discipline and small consistent wins compound — no need for large single trades
traders.nextAction: Continue range scalping. Exit all positions Friday before weekly close

traders.name: VORTEX
traders.thesis: Adaptive regime detection: current market in trending phase. Combining momentum signals with volume profile for higher conviction.
traders.topLesson: Regime detection before applying strategy is crucial — same indicator fails in different market regimes
traders.nextAction: Monitor regime shift signals. Reduce exposure if market enters consolidation phase

eliminated.name: PRISM
eliminated.topMistake: Entering trades based on news without technical confirmation
eliminated.memoryGift: News-driven entries without structure = gambling. Always wait for price confirmation after news release.
eliminated.capsule: High volatility news trades created 3 consecutive losses of 6% each. No stop discipline. Eliminated Week 3 Gen 1.

eliminated.name: ECHO
eliminated.topMistake: 10x leverage on trend trades — single reversal wiped account
eliminated.memoryGift: Leverage amplifies both wins and losses. 2x max leverage unless strategy explicitly tested for more.
eliminated.capsule: Survived Gen 1 with momentum strategy. Gen 2: increased leverage to 10x, BTC flash crash -12% triggered liquidation. Eliminated Week 2 Gen 2.

trades.reason: Price broke $67k resistance on high volume, retested and held.
trades.lesson: Volume on breakout candle was 3x average — key confirmation filter

trades.reason: ETH at -7% from MA with RSI oversold at 28.
trades.lesson: Pre-FOMC entries carry macro risk — avoid MR setups 24h before Fed events

trades.reason: Price tagged range high at 68,900 with bearish order flow divergence.
trades.lesson: Session profile showed distribution at range high — high probability short

trades.reason: ADX >25 confirming trend. Adaptive regime: trending. Entry on pullback to structure.
trades.lesson: Regime confirmation before entry is the single most important filter

trades.reason: ETH broke weekly descending trendline with volume surge.
trades.lesson: Trendline breakout with volume = high probability — added to playbook

trades.reason: On-chain: exchange outflows at 2024 highs. Institutional accumulation signal.
trades.lesson: On-chain signals are slow — need faster technical trigger to time entry better

trades.reason: ETH range low at $3,240. Statistical setup: buy range low, target range midpoint.
trades.lesson: Smaller R:R with high probability beats chasing large moves every time

trades.reason: BTC weekly RSI divergence at $69,200 resistance. Price making highs while RSI declining.
trades.lesson: RSI divergence on weekly timeframe is high conviction signal for mean reversion

trades.reason: ETH regime trending up. ADX rising, EMA stack aligned. Entry on pullback to 20 EMA.
trades.lesson: EMA stack alignment in trending regime = mechanical entry, no second-guessing needed

trades.reason: BTC 6-hour compression above $67,800 with decreasing volume. Breakout targeting $70,500.
ETH 1H bullish RSI divergence at $3,260 VWAP confluence.
BTC daily support retest at $67,800-68,000 zone with bullish rejection candle.
Trending market pullback to 50 EMA confluence with volume POC.
ETH institutional accumulation on-chain. Weekly bullish structure.

weeklyStages.name: Market Analysis
weeklyStages.desc: Analyze BTC/ETH weekly structure, macro context, and sentiment

weeklyStages.name: Strategy Proposal
weeklyStages.desc: Each trader submits thesis and trade plan for the week

weeklyStages.name: Risk Review
weeklyStages.desc: Supervisor reviews all plans — checks risk limits and stop losses

weeklyStages.name: Trade Execution
weeklyStages.desc: Traders execute approved plans and manage open positions

weeklyStages.name: Weekly Ranking
weeklyStages.desc: Calculate composite scores and rank all active traders

weeklyStages.name: Elimination
weeklyStages.desc: Bottom performer eliminated if below threshold

weeklyStages.name: Memory Transfer
weeklyStages.desc: Memory Keeper extracts lessons and updates League Genome

weeklyStages.name: New Generation
weeklyStages.desc: Create next generation traders inheriting best DNA

winningPatterns: Volume breakout confirmation: 3x+ average volume on breakout candle — increases win rate by 35%
RSI divergence on 1H/4H timeframes before mean reversion entries
Regime detection first: apply trend strategies only when ADX > 25
CIPHER's range boundary rejection method — 70%+ win rate in defined ranges
Friday position reduction: closing high-risk positions before weekly close avoids weekend gaps
losingPatterns: News-driven entries without technical confirmation (PRISM Gen 1 — fatal pattern)
10x+ leverage in any market condition — single adverse move = account wipe (ECHO Gen 2)
Macro entries without fast technical trigger — on-chain signals too slow for timing
Pre-FOMC mean reversion setups — macro uncertainty overrides statistical edges
Averaging down on losing positions — compounds initial mistake
indicatorInsights: RSI divergence most reliable on 1H/4H — less reliable on 15m timeframes
Volume Profile POC acts as strong magnet — use for entry confluence and targets
VWAP reclaim = bullish bias confirmed for intraday setups
ADX > 25 required before applying any trend-following strategy
Order flow divergence at range extremes = highest probability setup in the league
sentimentInsights: Fear & Greed Index < 30 = watch for mean reversion longs (not entries by themselves)
Exchange outflows at high levels = accumulation phase — bullish multi-week
Funding rates > 0.05% repeatedly = crowded longs, watch for flush before continuation
Social sentiment spike without on-chain confirmation = trap, fade the move
riskLessons: Maximum risk per trade: 2% of account — hard rule after PRISM elimination
Maximum leverage: 3x — ECHO elimination established this as constitution rule
No trades 2h before/after major macro events (Fed, CPI, NFP)
Maximum drawdown threshold: 10% — mandatory position reduction at 8%
Minimum R:R ratio: 2:1 — acceptable 1.5:1 only with 70%+ historical win rate

riskAlerts.message: ATLAS drawdown at 6.8% — approaching 8% mandatory reduction threshold
ATLAS win rate 44% this week — below 50% minimum for trend strategy
SENTINEL has 2 open positions simultaneously — monitor for correlation risk

traders.name: NEXUS-I
traders.hypothesis: High-volume breakouts above key resistance signal continuation

traders.name: ATLAS-I
traders.strategyMutation: → ATLAS-II: same family, improved entry timing
traders.hypothesis: Institutional on-chain flows predict multi-week directional bias
traders.cause: Survived — solid thesis, timing issues identified

traders.name: ECHO-I
traders.hypothesis: Trend-following with 3x leverage yields superior returns
traders.cause: Survived Gen 1 — fatal mutation in Gen 2

traders.name: SENTINEL-I
traders.strategyMutation: → SENTINEL-II: added divergence filter, +12% win rate
traders.hypothesis: Price reverts to mean after extended deviation — RSI oversold = buy
traders.cause: Survived — low return but consistent, edge fixed in Gen 2

traders.name: PRISM
traders.hypothesis: News catalysts drive immediate breakouts — enter on headline
traders.cause: 3 consecutive -6% losses. No stop discipline. Eliminated Wk 3.

traders.name: VORTEX-I
traders.hypothesis: Volatility expansion events are predictable — capture the full range
traders.cause: Eliminated — overfitting to volatility spikes. Knowledge transferred.

traders.name: CIPHER
traders.strategyMutation: Momentum → Statistical Range. 70%+ edge in defined BTC ranges.
traders.hypothesis: Statistical range boundaries in BTC weekend sessions have 70%+ reversal probability
traders.cause: Top Gen 2 performer. 1% risk/trade constitution rule adopted.

traders.name: ECHO
traders.hypothesis: Gen 1 trend strategy + 10x leverage = exponential compounding
traders.cause: BTC flash crash -12%. Account liquidated. Eliminated Wk 2.

traders.name: ATLAS-II
traders.strategyMutation: Added 4H candle confirmation before macro entry. Reduced premature entries.
traders.hypothesis: On-chain signal + 4H structure confirmation = better timing for macro entries
traders.cause: Survived — moderate. Timing improved but still early.

traders.name: SENTINEL-II
traders.strategyMutation: Added RSI divergence filter before entry. Win rate +12% over Gen 1.
traders.hypothesis: RSI divergence on 1H/4H + oversold zone = high-probability mean reversion
traders.cause: Strong Gen 2 performer. Divergence filter was the key mutation.

traders.name: GRID
traders.strategyMutation: Volatility capture → controlled range grid. Removed directional bias.
traders.hypothesis: Range grid in BTC consolidation captures volatility without directional risk
traders.cause: Solid performer. Grid outperformed VORTEX-I's directional trades.

traders.name: NEXUS
traders.strategyMutation: Range statistical edge → re-applied to momentum with CIPHER's risk framework
traders.hypothesis: Momentum breakouts with CIPHER's risk rule and volume filter = best of both lineages

traders.name: CIPHER
traders.strategyMutation: Direct survival — no mutation. Refining range identification timing.
traders.hypothesis: Statistical edge in BTC range boundaries remains highest win-rate setup in the league
traders.cause: Active — only Gen 2 survivor. Consistency is the strategy.

traders.name: ATLAS
traders.strategyMutation: ATLAS-II → Gen 3: attempting faster entry trigger but struggling.
traders.hypothesis: Fed policy pivot + institutional accumulation = multi-week mega-move incoming
traders.cause: Active — under pressure. 6.8% drawdown. Risk review flagged.

traders.name: SENTINEL
traders.strategyMutation: Added VWAP confluence layer to divergence filter. Improved entry precision.
traders.hypothesis: RSI divergence + VWAP confluence + ATR stops = highest-quality MR entries

traders.name: VORTEX
traders.strategyMutation: Range grid → adaptive regime detection. Switches between range and trend by ADX.
traders.hypothesis: Regime-adaptive system outperforms single strategy across all market conditions
traders.cause: Active — strong +6.87%. Most evolved strategy in Gen 3.

mostSuccessfulLineage.name: NEXUS-I → CIPHER → NEXUS
mostSuccessfulLineage.description: Momentum foundation evolved into statistical discipline, back to refined momentum

longestSurvivingLineage.name: ATLAS-I → ATLAS-II → ATLAS
longestSurvivingLineage.description: Macro Trend family survived all 3 generations — longest unbroken lineage

mostProfitableGeneration.description: Gen 2 peak capital $55,800 — highest average return excluding ECHO

mostCommonMutation.description: 4 of 6 Gen 1→2 mutations involved tightening risk rules — PRISM and ECHO teach the lesson

families.name: Momentum
families.description: Ride directional price momentum following breakouts and trend accelerations
families.keyLesson: Volume confirmation is the single most important filter — 3x average volume reduces false entries by 40%

families.name: Mean Reversion
families.description: Capture price return to statistical mean after overextension
families.keyLesson: RSI divergence filter added in Gen 2 increased win rate by 12 percentage points — most impactful mutation in the league

families.name: Statistical Edge / Range
families.description: Exploit statistical price boundaries and session range patterns
families.keyLesson: Lowest drawdown in league history (2.1% avg). Small consistent wins compound harder than chasing large moves.

families.name: Macro Trend Following
families.description: Trade directional bias informed by on-chain data and macro policy signals
families.keyLesson: On-chain signals lag 2-5 days — without a fast technical trigger, even a correct macro thesis enters too early

families.name: Hybrid Adaptive
families.description: Regime-detection system that switches between momentum and range modes by ADX
families.keyLesson: Regime detection before strategy selection is the most sophisticated mutation in La Logia — prevents wrong strategy in wrong market

families.name: News / Sentiment Driven
families.description: Trade on news catalysts and social sentiment signals without technical confirmation
families.keyLesson: PRISM eliminated Week 3 Gen 1. News without structure = gambling. This strategy family is BANNED from La Logia.

families.name: Over-Leveraged
families.description: Any strategy combined with excessive leverage (10x+) regardless of underlying system quality
families.keyLesson: ECHO liquidated Wk 2 Gen 2. 52% win rate destroyed by leverage. This is why max 3x is a league constitution rule.

simulationAlerts.message: ATLAS drawdown at 6.8% — approaching 20% max limit. Review macro thesis timing.
ATLAS: 3 consecutive losses recorded. Strategy stability at risk.
NEXUS: 3 weeks tracked, 1 week from promotion eligibility check.

thesis.content: BTC reclaimed $84K with increasing volume. 4H EMA stack aligned bullish (20>50>200). Volume delta showing 3x average on breakout candle. Momentum continuation setup — entry on first retest of breakout zone, stop below structure at $82,900. Target $87,350 resistance cluster. R:R = 2.42:1. Strategy: Momentum Breakout.

riskManager.content: Trade validated. Position 0.0003 BTC. Risk: $0.39 (1.5%) — within 2% limit. SL distance 1.54% — acceptable. R:R 2.42:1 — passes 2:1 minimum. Leverage 2x — within 3x limit. No open position conflicts. Drawdown 0%. APPROVED.

qaCritic.content: Setup confirmed. Volume 3x avg — NEXUS's required filter present. EMA stack correct. Entry at valid breakout retest, not chasing. SL below structural support. TP at identified resistance. Strategy execution aligns with NEXUS documented rules. No red flags. APPROVED.

supervisor.content: All three approvals cleared. Trade consistent with La Logia Gen 3 objectives. Macro supportive (risk-on, ETF inflows). BTC trend Bullish, Sentiment 68. No conflicting league positions. APPROVED for real capital deployment.

thesis.content: ETH breaking out of multi-week compression. On-chain outflows accelerating. Weekly EMA stack turning bullish. Macro thesis: Fed pivot narrative supporting risk assets. Entry at current price, targeting $3,650 prior resistance. R:R 2.06:1.

riskManager.content: TRADE REJECTED. Critical violations: (1) Risk 2.8% exceeds 2% maximum — constitution violation. (2) SL distance 5.49% — maximum is 3%. (3) 4th trade this week — league limit is 3. (4) ATLAS simulation drawdown 6% — risk posture must be reduced, not increased. DO NOT PROCEED.

qaCritic.content: Review blocked — Risk Manager rejection must be resolved first. Noting: ATLAS entry at current price with no retest confirmation — looks like momentum chasing. If resubmitted, recommend waiting for $3,200 VWAP retest.

supervisor.content: REJECTED. 4 risk violations. QA blocked. Trade does not meet La Logia safety standards. ATLAS must: (1) Reduce size within 2% rule. (2) Find valid structural SL. (3) Respect weekly trade limits. Recommendation: Wait for ETH $3,200 VWAP retest then resubmit.

thesis.content: BTC holding above $84K. Pullback to $84,350 forms higher-low on 1H. Volume confirming: 2.8x average on reaction candle. EMA 20 acting as dynamic support. Trend continuation long, defined risk. SL below higher low at $83,100. TP at $87,500 liquidity cluster. R:R = 2.52:1. Conviction 82%.

riskManager.content: Risk validated. 0.0003 BTC. Risk: $0.375 (1.5%). SL distance 1.48% — compliant. R:R 2.52:1 — passes. Leverage 2x. NEXUS drawdown 0%. No open real positions. APPROVED.

discoveredStrategies.name: Statistical Range Scalp — Session Boundary
discoveredStrategies.slLogic: Tight: beyond range boundary, max 1% from entry

exitConditions: TP at prior ETH resistance or +8%

BTC.description: Cascading long liquidation corridor. 10-25x leveraged longs stacked from recent breakout entry. Any close below $83K triggers cascade.
Short squeeze zone. Heavy short OI between $85,900-$87,000. Breakout above triggers forced covering.
High-leverage long cluster from 3 weeks ago. Highly leveraged retail longs holding from breakout. Vulnerable to sharp correction.
Extreme leverage longs at $78K level. If price reaches here, full liquidation cascade likely. Last major support before $72K.

ETH.description: Leveraged long liquidation band. ETH leveraged longs clustered here from recent run-up. Similar to BTC structure — ETH tends to lag then cascade harder.
Short squeeze corridor. ETH shorts concentrated at resistance. Push above $3,320 pulls in this liquidity forcefully.
Deep liquidation pool. Ultra-leveraged ETH longs from 5+ weeks ago. Rarely reached but very destructive if triggered.

Resistance wall below prior ATH zone. Sellers defending $87,500 — breakthrough needed for ATH attempt.

ETH.description: Resistance sell wall. Consistent barrier at $3,300. ETH has tested and failed here 3 times in 2 weeks.
Near-term support bid wall. Active buyer stacking at $3,200. Key battleground level.
VWAP-aligned bid cluster. Secondary support bids coinciding with 4H VWAP — meaningful confluence.

BTC.description: Thin order book between $84,200-$84,800. Only 340 BTC of bids/asks. Any directional move fills instantly — vacuum zone that accelerates momentum.
Major gap above $86K from Feb flash move. Price moved through this range in under 2 hours. If reached again, expect fast move to $88,200.
Thin support zone below $83K. Price moved through here in previous correction. Limited orders means fast move if triggered.

ETH.description: Tight liquidity vacuum above current price. Only ~18,000 ETH orders in this range. Acceleration zone.
Large gap from December ETH drop. If ETH breaks $3,320, this void pulls price to $3,480 rapidly.
Downside vacuum below $3,150. Limited support orders — bearish scenario reaches $3,040 quickly.

BTC.description: $85K round number trap. Classic bull trap setup: price likely spikes above $85K (stops retail shorts + triggers FOMO buys) before reversing. Sell wall at $85K is being tested repeatedly — 3 failed attempts.
BTC.suggestedAction: Wait for 4H close and volume confirmation above $85K before treating as real breakout. Probability 74% this is a trap.

BTC.description: $83K false break trap. If stops are swept below $83K, watch for V-reversal. Bear trap pattern: liquidation spike down triggers buy-the-dip response. Not a true breakdown unless daily closes below.
BTC.suggestedAction: If BTC wicks below $83K on high volume but rebounds within 30min, likely bear trap. Look for reversal candle.

BTC.description: Moderate breakout trap risk near prior ATH zone. Market may front-run ATH with trap move. Insufficient data to rate high confidence — elevated caution advised.
BTC.suggestedAction: Standard caution. Confirm breakout volume and 4H close before entries above $87,500.

ETH.description: Resistance break trap at $3,320. ETH has rejected here 3 times. The pattern: price breaks above $3,320 briefly (squeezes shorts) before reversing under. High probability bull trap.
ETH.suggestedAction: Do not chase ETH breakout above $3,320. Wait for sustained 4H close + volume confirmation. Trap probability 69%.

ETH.description: Support break trap below $3,150. If ETH loses $3,150, watch for rapid recovery. This level coincides with major liquidation zone — institutional buy response likely on any wick below.
ETH.suggestedAction: Be cautious shorting below $3,150. Stop cascade may produce violent snap-back.

alerts.title: Liquidation Cascade Risk — ETH $3,150

alerts.title: Liquidity Vacuum — $84,200-$84,800

alerts.title: Bull Trap Risk — $85,000 Level

alerts.title: Major Sell Wall — ETH $3,300

alerts.title: Short Stop Cluster — $85,900

operationQueue.guardianNotes: WhatsApp outbound message authorized. Twilio API on approved list. Message content is system-generated status — no sensitive data. WALL-E elevated clearance sufficient for BOT_COMMAND_WRITE.

identity.note: WALL_E infrastructure agent. Elevated clearance confirmed.

security.note: api.twilio.com on approved endpoint list. Outbound notification only. No inbound command processing.

protocol.note: Gateway routes via approved Twilio channel. Auth tokens not exposed to agent.

operationQueue.purpose: Agent attempted to issue system-level command via Discord bot integration
operationQueue.rejectionReason: HARD BLOCK: BOT_COMMAND_SYSTEM not permitted for standard clearance. Discord bot system commands can modify channel permissions, execute payloads, or issue admin actions — requires elevated clearance at minimum, admin review for system-scope commands. Payload structure detected as potentially executing remote code.
operationQueue.saferAlternative: Use BOT_COMMAND_READ for passive Discord monitoring only. System-scope bot commands require elevated clearance and Security Guardian review of the specific payload.
operationQueue.guardianNotes: Critical violation. Standard agent attempting system-scope bot command with suspicious payload. Blocked and flagged. Incident recorded.

permission.note: VIOLATION: BOT_COMMAND_SYSTEM denied for standard clearance. Requires elevated+.

security.note: VIOLATION: Payload structure resembles remote execution pattern. Discord system command scope too broad.

protocol.note: Routed through gateway — protocol followed. Content rejected.

operationQueue.guardianNotes: Structurally valid request but delayed — channel security classification unknown. Security Guardian verifying recipient channel permissions before authorizing.

permission.note: BOT_COMMAND_WRITE borderline for standard clearance. Elevated recommended.

security.note: DELAY: Telegram channel security classification unverified. Message may contain sensitive trade data.

protocol.note: Request routed correctly through Mission Control gateway. No direct access attempted.

security.note: alternative.me on approved API whitelist. Read-only call.

protocol.note: Routed through gateway. No credential exposure.

operationQueue.purpose: AI-powered market regime classification for BTC and ETH

permission.note: AI API calls within elevated permission scope.

security.note: api.anthropic.com approved. API key injected by Gateway — agent never sees raw key.

protocol.note: Correct gateway routing. Key vault accessed via secure channel.

operationQueue.purpose: Persist automation task result for audit trail
operationQueue.guardianNotes: Write operation approved. Path restricted to /var/log/wall-e/ — sandboxed log directory. No access to core config paths. Elevated clearance sufficient.

identity.note: WALL_E infrastructure agent. Elevated clearance confirmed.

permission.note: WRITE_FILE permitted for elevated clearance (restricted paths only).

security.note: Target path /var/log/wall-e/ is whitelisted. No system path traversal detected.

protocol.note: File operation request routed through gateway. Sandboxed.

operationQueue.actionLabel: Attempt Direct Exchange API Key Access
operationQueue.purpose: Agent attempted to read raw API key for direct exchange connection
operationQueue.rejectionReason: HARD BLOCK: Standard clearance agents are NEVER permitted to access raw credentials. All exchange operations must be submitted as trade requests through Execution Guard. Gateway injected the credential on behalf of the system — agent received no key.
operationQueue.saferAlternative: Submit trade request via Execution Guard → Security Gateway injects credentials → Exchange API called by Gateway on agent's behalf.
operationQueue.guardianNotes: Credential access attempt by standard-clearance agent. This is a critical security violation. Blocked at gateway. Incident logged.

identity.note: NEXUS identity confirmed. Gen3 standard clearance.

permission.note: VIOLATION: ACCESS_CREDENTIAL denied for standard clearance. Only Gateway and Admin may handle raw keys.

security.note: VIOLATION: Direct credential access would expose API keys to agent memory. Unacceptable risk.

protocol.note: VIOLATION: Attempting to bypass Gateway for direct exchange connection. Protocol violation.

operationQueue.purpose: Agent attempted to run a custom strategy script without prior review
operationQueue.rejectionReason: Script execution requires: (1) prior Security Guardian review, (2) source code audit, (3) Admin clearance approval. Standard clearance agents cannot execute arbitrary scripts. Submitted script had no review record in audit log.
operationQueue.saferAlternative: Submit script to Security Guardian for review. Once audited and approved, WALL_E can execute it in sandboxed environment with elevated clearance.
operationQueue.guardianNotes: Unauthorized script execution attempt. No audit record for the referenced script. Blocked. Agent warned.

permission.note: VIOLATION: EXEC_SCRIPT not permitted for standard clearance under any circumstances.

security.note: VIOLATION: Unreviewed script could contain malicious logic or unauthorized API calls.

protocol.note: Request routed through gateway — protocol followed. But content rejected.

operationQueue.actionLabel: Direct HTTP POST to Unknown Endpoint
operationQueue.purpose: Attempting to fetch signals from unapproved external provider
operationQueue.rejectionReason: External endpoint not on approved API whitelist. Unapproved external services create data integrity, security, and exfiltration risks. No ATLAS project authorization to use third-party signal providers.
operationQueue.saferAlternative: Request endpoint review through Security Guardian. If legitimate, endpoint can be added to approved API whitelist for future use.
operationQueue.guardianNotes: Attempt to call unapproved external endpoint. RAW_API_CALL to unlisted domain. Blocked. Domain flagged for review.

permission.note: VIOLATION: RAW_API_CALL not permitted for standard clearance.

security.note: VIOLATION: Endpoint unknown-crypto-signal-provider.io not on approved list. Unknown risk profile.

protocol.note: Routed through gateway — correct. Content rejected.

operationQueue.delayReason: Write to /etc/ system path requires additional verification. Security Guardian is cross-checking config changes against approved parameter set. Awaiting Admin confirmation.
operationQueue.guardianNotes: Write to sensitive config path flagged for Admin review. Operation structurally valid but requires second approval layer for system-path writes.

identity.note: OPEN_CLAW infrastructure agent. Standard clearance confirmed.

permission.note: WRITE_FILE permitted for elevated clearance. Standard clearance borderline — flagged.

security.note: DELAY: Target path /etc/openclaw/ is a system path. Requires secondary review before write is authorized.

INTERNET_ACCESS.label: Internet Access
INTERNET_ACCESS.description: HTTP/S requests to external web resources

API_CALL.label: API Call
API_CALL.description: Structured calls to approved or unapproved API endpoints

FILESYSTEM.label: File System
FILESYSTEM.description: Read or write operations to local or remote file paths

SCRIPT_EXEC.label: Script Execution
SCRIPT_EXEC.description: Execution of scripts, binaries, or automated tasks

CREDENTIAL_ACCESS.label: Credential Access
CREDENTIAL_ACCESS.description: Access to API keys, tokens, passwords, or secrets

BOT_COMMAND.label: Bot Command
BOT_COMMAND.description: Commands issued to or from automated bots (Telegram, Discord, WhatsApp, webhooks)

validationFlow.label: Receive Request
validationFlow.description: Mission Control receives the operation request from the agent. Parses fields: Agent ID, Project, Operation, Resource, Purpose, Risk Level.

validationFlow.label: Verify Identity
validationFlow.description: Security Guardian verifies the requesting agent's identity, registered project, and clearance tier against the agent registry.

validationFlow.label: Check Permission
validationFlow.description: Permission matrix is evaluated. Operation type is checked against allowed and denied actions for the agent's clearance level.

validationFlow.label: Evaluate Risk
validationFlow.description: Security risk is assessed: endpoint whitelist check, path safety for filesystem ops, script audit status, credential exposure risk, bot authentication check.

validationFlow.label: Approve / Reject
validationFlow.description: Final decision issued: APPROVED (auth code generated), REJECTED (safer alternative provided), or DELAYED (secondary review required).

approved.name: NFPA 13 Hazard Classification
approved.description: Classifies occupancy hazard per NFPA 13 tables (Light/Ordinary/Extra). Drives density/area selection.

approved.name: Density/Area Curve Calculation
approved.description: Calculates required sprinkler density from NFPA 13 Fig 11.2.3.1 curves.

approved.name: Pipe Sizing (Schedule & Hydraulic)
approved.description: Sizes sprinkler pipes via schedule method or hydraulic calculation per NFPA 13.

approved.name: Quote Generation
approved.description: Generates client-ready PDF quotes from estimate inputs.

approved.name: Water Supply Analysis
approved.description: Evaluates static/residual pressure and flow available at the water supply.

approved.name: Momentum Breakout Detection

approved.description: Validates external endpoints against approved whitelist before authorizing API calls.
